title: <div>  2.  Pluto (Naoki Urasawa) – A reimagining of Osamu Tezuka’s Astro Boy tha
content:
2. Pluto (Naoki Urasawa) – A reimagining of Osamu Tezuka’s Astro Boy that explores human identity, morality, and the nature of conflict.
3. Blue Period (Tsubasa Yamaguchi) – A manga about artistic drive and self-discovery, following a student who dedicates himself obsessively to painting.

Your choice should be based on what resonates with your vision—whether it’s the intensity of personal struggle, the philosophical depth, or the sheer power of visual storytelling.

Breaking Down Your Inspirations (Beat-by-Beat Analysis)

To truly learn from these works, let’s break them down into story beats. Each follows a dramatic structure, which we will later use to shape your own story.

1. Whiplash – Beat Breakdown

Beat Description
Opening Image Andrew, a talented but insecure drummer, plays alone in a music conservatory practice room. Fletcher enters—an immediate, intimidating presence.
Setup Andrew is driven but lacks confidence. Fletcher recruits him for his elite jazz band.
Inciting Incident Fletcher humiliates Andrew in rehearsal, throwing a chair at him.
context: From document: The Graphic Novel Writing Guide for Derek: Inspiration, Structure… — chunk 2 (chunk 2/22)
